events.description: This Employment Agreement is entered into between Sandbourne Industries Inc., a corporation incorporated under the laws of Alberta ("Employer"), and Jordan Fairweather, an individual residing in Calgary, Alberta ("Employee"), effective as of April 1, 2026

deadlines.date: no later than March 31 of each calendar year following the performance year
deadlines.description: The Employee shall be eligible to participate in the Employer's annual performance bonus plan, with a target bonus of 20% of base salary, payable no later than March 31 of each calendar year following the performance year, subject to the Employee remaining employed as of the payment date

deadlines.date: no later than the effective date of termination

deadlines.date: 15 business days after receipt of such written notice to cure the deficiency to th
deadlines.description: The Employee shall have 15 business days after receipt of such written notice to cure the deficiency to the reasonable satisfaction of the Employer

obligations.description: The Employer shall employ the Employee as Director of Operations
The Employee shall report directly to the Chief Executive Officer of Sandbourne Industries Inc. and shall perform such duties

The Employee shall devote substantially all of the Employee's working time and attention to the performance of duties under this Agreement and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Employer, engage in any outside employment or business activities that conflict with the Employee's obligations hereunder
The Employer shall pay the Employee a base salary of $185,000 per annum, payable in equal semi-monthly instalments

The Employee shall be eligible to participate in the Employer's annual performance bonus plan, with a target bonus of 20% of base salary, payable no later than March 31 of each calendar year following the performance year
The Employer shall provide the Employee with group benefits coverage, including extended health, dental, and life insurance
The Employer shall provide the Employee with written notice of termination without cause no later than the effective date of termination
The Employee shall provide the Employer with no less than 30 days prior written notice of the Employee's intention to resign
During the notice period, the Employee shall continue to perform the Employee's duties and shall cooperate with the transition of the Employee's responsibilities
If the Employer alleges cause based on performance-related conduct, the Employer shall first provide the Employee with written notice specifying the alleged deficiency in detail
The Employee shall have 15 business days after receipt of such written notice to cure the deficiency

During the term of employment and for a period of 12 months following termination, the Employee shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit or attempt to solicit any customer of Sandbourne Industries Inc. with whom the Employee had material contact during the 24 months preceding termination

During the term of employment and for a period of 6 months following termination, the Employee shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit, hire, or attempt to hire any employee of Sandbourne Industries Inc
The Employee agrees to keep confidential all Confidential Information of Sandbourne Industries Inc. both during and after the term of employment
The Employee must immediately notify the Employer in writing upon becoming aware of any actual or suspected unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information

The Employer shall make all deductions required by law, including income tax, Canada Pension Plan contributions, and Employment Insurance premiums, from amounts payable to the Employee under this Agreement

conditions.description: The Employer may terminate the Employee's employment without cause by providing the Employee with working notice or pay in lieu of notice equal to
The Employer may terminate this Agreement immediately for cause without notice or payment in lieu of notice upon: (i) material breach of this Agreement by the Employee
The Employee shall have 15 business days after receipt of such written notice to cure the deficiency to the reasonable satisfaction of the Employer
If the Employee fails to cure within the 15-business-day cure period, the Employer may terminate for cause
The obligation of confidentiality shall survive termination of this Agreement indefinitely with respect to trade secrets and for 5 years with respect to other Confidential Information

transfers.description: The Employer shall pay the Employee a base salary of $185,000 per annum, payable in equal semi-monthly instalments in accordance with the Employer's payroll practices
